title: Graph-based benchmarking for MOF synthesizability and stability proxies
rationale: Still the lead because execution is far ahead of the rest of the board: mapped data, fixed split, descriptor baselines, graph artifact, graph-path baselines, true MPNN attempts, and a descriptor-plus-graph hybrid comparison now all exist. Family-specific skeptic did not produce a strong downgrade, but graph advantage is materially weakened because descriptor_hgb_v1 still leads on the primary metric even after graph and hybrid follow-ups.
next_action: Freeze H10 as a benchmark-comparison result and shift the next exploratory push to H4 dataset verification unless a sharply justified new graph hypothesis appears.

title: TDA for early detection of resistant-state transitions in cancer single-cell data
rationale: Family-specific skeptic did not find a strong downgrade, so H4 stays high-upside. The dataset situation is now materially better: there is a fast open resistance-state benchmark route and a stronger patient-derived follow-up route. The first executable path is now clear enough to move from scouting into scaffold planning.
next_action: Build the first H4 benchmark scaffold around GSE164897, while keeping GSE120575 / scCT-DB as patient-derived phase-2 routes.

title: Early-warning PH predictor of SOC electrode degradation trajectories
rationale: H20 survives as a narrow longitudinal early-warning candidate. The family-specific skeptic pass did not add a new downgrade, but the earlier verification evidence still says the broad SOC+PH framing is already crowded.
next_action: Test whether a longitudinal early-warning gap actually remains open on a defensible time-separated benchmark.

title: Koopman-style slow-mode analysis for LLPS-relevant IDP trajectory data
rationale: Family-specific skeptic produced a real downgrade here. H1 is still conceptually interesting, but it now has both a data-route problem and stronger evidence that adjacent LLPS tooling is already well developed.
next_action: Search for open LLPS trajectory datasets or reusable condensate simulations.

title: Benchmarking persistent-homology descriptors for metallic-glass transition and deformation proxies
rationale: Family-specific skeptic also downgraded H2. It remains executable, but the evidence is increasingly consistent that this is benchmark work in a crowded prior-art zone rather than a leading discovery bet.
next_action: Confirm whether the chosen proxy target is scientifically defensible and whether the benchmark is worth carrying at all.
